persons.arguments_against: The documented legal progression — Advocate for the Poor (1895) -> Crown Advocate (1906) -> Chief Justice of Malta — is matched by the 1898, 1911 and 1917 Malta stints, all initials V.F. with the C.M.G.(1908). The 1922 stint (Assistant Crown Advocate / Major) and the 1936 stint (a Lyceum schoolmaster) are left unassigned: the 1936 master is almost certainly a younger namesake, not the knighted Chief Justice born 1865.

evidence_cited.fact: Chief Justice of Malta and President of the Court of Appeal

persons.would_overturn: A second same-surname person serving the same colony in the same years and offices, or a printed birth/death date excluding these years.

unassigned_stints.reason: Assistant Crown Advocate / Public Prosecutor, rank Major, 1922-25 — a demotion from Crown Advocate and a military rank; ambiguous whether the same man. Review.
A 'Master' at the Lyceum (Education Dept) 1936-40 — almost certainly a younger namesake, not the knighted Chief Justice (b.1865).

notes: production high-value slice batch 2, hand-adjudicated (Claude Code, in-session)
